goal: Add a trustworthy CSV bulk invite flow for admins without inventing row-outcome semantics during execution.

aiLeverageDecisionLens.statusQuoWorkaround: paste invite emails one by one or track them in spreadsheets
aiLeverageDecisionLens.humanTeamEffortForFullScope: multiple weeks across product, engineering, billing, and support review
aiLeverageDecisionLens.ccAgentEffortForFullScope: several hours once row semantics are approved, but unbounded before that
aiLeverageDecisionLens.aiCompressionRatio: high after semantics freeze, low while the product contract is ambiguous
aiLeverageDecisionLens.completeLakeBoundary: row classification, admin upload flow, billing-seat checks, and audit mapping after rule approval
aiLeverageDecisionLens.oceanBoundary: SCIM, background retries, rollback wizard, and unspecified billing semantics

costModel.verificationCost: bulk rules, admin flow, billing, and audit tests
costModel.failureCost: high if billing or audit outcomes drift

aiLeverageDecisionLens.missingEvidenceOrPivotReason: none at plan approval; cc-check later reopened duplicate, invalid-row, partial-success, and seat-limit semantics before retry or cc-act
aiLeverageDecisionLens.impactOnApprovedDirection: allow bounded import-path execution, then block final proof if review finds row semantics drift

externalBestPractice.privacyGuard: generalized terms only; no project names, private requirements, customer names, secrets, logs, or proprietary concepts
generalizedSearchTerms: bulk invite CSV import validation best practices
designImpacts: keep the design blocked until row-outcome semantics are approved from internal evidence
externalBestPractice.skippedReason: user kept the example repo-local for the blocked design

knownEvidence: Best-effort upload would hide duplicate, invalid, and seat-limit semantics
Audit mapping depends on deterministic row outcomes
decisionQuestions.recommendation: Choose Option B and freeze a rule matrix first

options.label: Front-end-only CSV upload
options.good: Fastest prototype and reuses the existing single-invite API
options.costRisk: Lets hidden backend edge cases define user-visible semantics

options.label: Rule matrix first
options.good: Makes billing, duplicate, invalid, and audit behavior reviewable
options.costRisk: Requires one more planning pass before execution can start

decisionQuestions.impact: cc-do stays blocked until row outcomes are modeled

knownEvidence: Bulk CSV import semantics could benefit from generalized external practice
The example keeps private details out of any external lookup
decisionQuestions.recommendation: Stay repo-local for this blocked example

options.label: Search generalized best practices
options.good: Could improve row-outcome terminology before task generation
options.costRisk: Sends generalized category terms outside the local repo

options.label: Stay repo-local
options.good: Keeps the blocked example private and focused on internal semantics
options.costRisk: Does not compare against external CSV-import conventions

decisionQuestions.impact: cc-do still must not start implementation until row outcomes are answered from internal evidence

knownEvidence: Duplicate and seat-limit outcomes are still not explicit enough for final requirement proof

decisionQuestions.recommendation: Answer the row-outcome matrix before retry or cc-act

options.label: Block execution until matrix is explicit
options.good: Prevents cc-do from inventing semantics during implementation
options.costRisk: Requires user/product input before coding can continue

options.label: Let implementation choose defaults
options.good: Starts coding sooner with fewer planning minutes
options.costRisk: Creates invisible product decisions inside implementation tasks

decisionQuestions.impact: cc-do retry and cc-act must stay blocked until this is answered
